member.office_address: 1306 Longworth House Office Building Washington DC 20515-0800

form_analysis.form_instructions_text: This request form is a joint request form for the Offices of Senators Kelly and Gallego. This form must be completed for your appropriations request to be considered. Please keep in mind that you cannot save your answers to the Microsoft Form and come back to it later. Be prepared to submit it in one sitting.

sections.description: Information about the individual and organization submitting the request.

fields.label: Full name
Title
Email
Direct Phone Number
Government Affairs Firm
Organization Name
Street Address
City
County
Zip Code

sections.description: General identification and classification of the programmatic request.

fields.label: Please select the relevant Appropriations subcommittee for your request:
options: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies
Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
Labor, Health and Human Services,�Education, and Related Agencies
Military Construction, Veterans Affairs, and Related Agencies
State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs
Transportation, Housing and Urban Development, and Related Agencies
fields.draft_value: Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
fields.rationale: NASA funding is under the jurisdiction of the CJS subcommittee.

fields.label: What is the program name or title of this request?
fields.rationale: Directly references the requested program name as specified in the reference values.

fields.label: Please select the type of request.
fields.rationale: The request context identifies this as a programmatic funding request.

fields.label: Under which federal agency and/or office does this program fall?�
fields.rationale: Directly references the federal agency as specified in the reference values.

fields.label: Under which account(s) does this federal program fall?
fields.rationale: Directly references the account name as specified in the reference values.

sections.description: Specific dollar amounts and legislative language requested for FY27 and historical funding context.

fields.label: Please indicate the funding level you are requesting for this federal program in FY27.
fields.rationale: Uses the specified funding ask amount from the reference values.

fields.label: Please indicate program funding level for FY26.
fields.help_text: Prior year enacted or requested level for FY26
fields.rationale: Uses the pre-filled value representing the FY2026 enacted level.

fields.label: Please indicate program funding level for FY24/FY25.
fields.help_text: Prior year enacted level for FY24 or FY25
fields.rationale: Uses the FY2025 enacted level as specified in the SMD Topline Funding History.

fields.label: Please indicate program funding level in the President's FY27 budget request.
fields.rationale: The reference values state that the President's Budget Request for FY2027 has not been released.

fields.label: Please provide the BILL language you wish to include in the FY27 appropriations bill.
fields.rationale: The request context specifies N/A for bill language.

fields.label: Was this bill language included in the FY26 appropriations bill?
fields.rationale: The request context indicates that no bill language is being requested for this programmatic funding increase.

fields.label: Please provide the REPORT language you wish to include in the FY27 appropriations bill.
fields.rationale: The request context specifies N/A for report language.

fields.label: Was this report language included in the FY26 appropriations bill?
fields.rationale: The request context indicates that no report language is being requested for this programmatic funding increase.

sections.description: Additional fields for requests falling under Research & Development or Defense accounts.

fields.label: What is the program name or title of this request?
fields.rationale: Duplicates earlier programmatic title field.

fields.label: Please select the type of request.
fields.rationale: Duplicates selection for request type.

fields.label: Under which federal agency and/or office does this program fall?�
fields.rationale: Duplicates earlier agency field.

fields.label: Under which account(s) does this federal program fall?
fields.rationale: Duplicates earlier account field.

fields.label: Please provide the appropriate Program Element (PE) line number for R&D accounts.
fields.rationale: The PE line number for the entire Science Mission Directorate is not provided in the reference context.

fields.label: Please indicate program funding level in the President's FY27 budget request.
fields.rationale: Duplicates earlier budget request field.

fields.label: Please indicate the funding level you are requesting ABOVE the amount included (or not included) in the President's budget request.
fields.rationale: This value cannot be determined because the President's FY27 budget request is currently unknown.

fields.label: Request Justification�
fields.help_text: Explain why this funding or language is needed.
fields.draft_value:
* This $9 billion request restores stability to NASA Science following the FY2026 disruptions, ensuring the U.S. remains the global leader in space exploration amidst rising competition from China.
* Space science is a fundamental public responsibility codified in Title 51; it advances national security and economic competitiveness in ways no single private entity can replicate.
* Funding supports peer-reviewed Decadal Survey priorities, providing a rigorous and nonpartisan basis for investments that deliver transformative scientific breakthroughs.
* Every dollar invested in NASA returns roughly three dollars to the economy, supporting a high-tech workforce and a steady demand for cutting-edge R&D.
fields.rationale: Justification uses advocacy talking points regarding U.S. leadership, competition with China, and economic returns, while referencing the $9B ask amount and FY2026 context.

fields.label: Arizona Connection
fields.help_text: Describe the impact or nexus to the state of Arizona.
fields.rationale: This field specifically asks for an Arizona connection, but the provided context and the Member (Rep. McBride) are for Delaware (DE-00). A null response is provided to avoid inaccurate or irrelevant data.

fields.label: How many requests for different programs/ projects does your organization plan on submitting?
fields.rationale: This is a user-specific operational question.

fields.label: If your organization is submitting multiple requests for different programs/ projects, please indicate the priority of this request compared to other requests you have submitted.�
options: Select this option if you are only submitting a request for one program/ project.
fields.rationale: The submitter's relative priority between multiple requests is not knowable from the provided context.

fields.label: Please list any Members of the House of Representatives to whom you are submitting the same request.
fields.draft_value: Reps. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA)
fields.rationale: References the existing letter leads identified in the congressional support context.

fields.label: Please provide any additional information that you would like our offices to know about your request.
fields.draft_value:
* NASA Science has a direct impact on Delaware, with over $18.7 million in total obligations across the district from FY2023 to FY2025.
* Significant local activity includes Blue Marble Space in Clayton, which has received over $14.6 million in grants for research on spaceflight biology and human health impacts.
* The University of Delaware in Newark is a key partner, receiving nearly $6 million in grants for critical work such as solar atmosphere simulations.
* This funding supports local STEM professionals and the training of the next generation of space scientists right here in the district.
fields.rationale: This field leverages the specific NASA spending data for DE-00, highlighting Blue Marble Space and the University of Delaware as key local recipients.

fields.label: Do you plan send documentation to support this request to appropriations@kelly.senate.gov and appropriations@gallego.senate.gov using the subject line template above? As a reminder, this is not a requirement.
fields.rationale: The submitter's intent to send additional documentation is not knowable from the provided context.

top_grants.description: The current extension continues the offer of a framework to cost-effectively integrate research using a suite of model organisms to target complementary questions in human spaceflight by providing to NASA post-bachelor apprentice scientists    the next generation of space biology leaders   trained as only blue marble space can  in laboratory and scientific excellence  in effective science communication and in the broader ethics related to their work. The overall objectives of this extension are to continue our successful work in identifying  and in the process lowering the cost in identifying  quantitatively  the effect of spaceflight environment on model organisms and how they translate to the human. This is achieved by providing a cost-effective talent pool and low overhead (blue marble space (bms) has a 10% fixed overhead rate)  communicate and integrate research findings with the ames space biology community by facilitating interlaboratory communication spear-headed by bms post-bachelor  apprentice scientists  (thereafter: post-bacs)  and continuing the training of these post-bacs in effective scientific  communication  and ethics skills. More specifically  this extension will continue the development of a cost-effective integrated scientific framework furthering NASA S research and educational needs. In particular: objective 1: investigate the loss of skeletal function at the molecular  physiological  and cellular level under spaceflight conditions and develop counter measures. Objective 2: investigate cell regeneration mechanisms under spaceflight conditions. Objective 3: investigate immune system adaptation to spaceflight. Objective 4: investigate cardiovascular and vascular adaptation to spaceflight. Objective 5: investigate eukaryotic cell adaptation to radiation exposure under spaceflight conditions. Objective 6: investigate organ and organism development under spaceflight conditions. Objective 7: investigate organ and organism development under spaceflight conditions. Examine microbial dispersal using NASA balloons  aircraft  and the international space station.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Investigating spaceflight effects on biology while training future space scientists.

top_grants.description: Unsolicited  comprehensive exploration of human physiological responses to prolonged.spaceflight using model organisms.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Researching human health impacts during prolonged space missions.

top_grants.description: The purpose of cld/rs is to conduct research to build critical knowledge that is presently lacking  develop tools that recast and organize existing and emerging knowledge in a way that facilitates its use in mission planning  and support the tech
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Creating essential knowledge and tools for planning future missions.

top_grants.description: eo14042 the influence of structure and turbulence on coronal and heliospheric dynamics - improved magnetohydrodynamic modeling with data-driven subgrid-scale effects
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Improving computer simulations of solar atmosphere dynamics
